Patch Set 2:

thanks, John. iirc, at some point it was said the migration doesn't work well with racer ?

Patch Set 2:

> thanks, John. iirc, at some point it was said the migration doesn't work well with racer ?

That's true. There are some issues around LDLM and undeclared operations. But the crash is from you added check to osd_object_release(). Do you still want this patch t land as is?

Patch Set 2:

basically this is incorrect condition that we leave an orphan in the filesystem. and this happens because of some issue in our code. from my point of view it's good to have such an assertion so that the issue doesn't happen silently. I'd like to understand why this happen during migration though..

parents.subject: LU-7579 osd: move ORPHAN/DEAD flag to OSD

commit.subject: LU-7680 mdd: put migrated object on the orphan list
commit.message:
LU-7680 mdd: put migrated object on the orphan list

do this unconditionally. mdd_finish_unlink() can't do this
properly as la_nlink cached in ma greater than 0. this
results in lost inodes (i.e. we still have an entry in OI,
but corresponding inode doesn't exist) and many messages
like:
LustreError: 3458:0:(osd_handler.c:3239:osd_object_ref_del())
 lustre-MDT0001-osd: nlink == 0 on [0x240000403:0x53b:0x0],
 maybe an upgraded file? (LU-3915)

the patch also adds a sanity check in osd_object_release() to
ensure that nobody is trying to leave non-destroyed object with
nlink = 0.

Change-Id: Iecfae75944854d8e9613431acb68ad17dfea90f0
Signed-off-by: Alex Zhuravlev <alexey.zhuravlev@intel.com>
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/18032
Reviewed-by: wangdi <di.wang@intel.com>
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: John L. Hammond <john.hammond@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
